reasoning: The AAA10Y spread often behaves locally like a random walk with long-term mean reversion. The baseline value for Nov 9, 2025, corresponds to Friday, Nov 7, 2025, when the spread closed at 1.16. This represents a recent spike (up from 1.05 on Oct 30) and is slightly above the 6-month average of around 1.10. Given this relatively high starting point, mean reversion makes a subsequent increase less likely than a decrease in the short term. The FRED linear trend model also predicts a decrease with P(increase) estimated at ~0.34 for the immediate horizon. Therefore, the probability of the spread being strictly higher than 1.16 is below 50%. As the forecast horizon extends, uncertainty increases and the probability tends closer to 50%.
